title: VisIter iteration graph (v1)
description:
Output shape of visiter.iterate — the documented data contract between iterate and to_dot.

Values in an iteration graph can be any hashable Python object (integers, strings, tuples, frozensets, ...). On the JSON wire they are encoded by the producer's serialization rules: native JSON types pass through unchanged, anything else is coerced to its `str()` form (the CLI uses `json.dump(default=str)`). Node keys are always strings — `str(value)` for in-memory dicts, the same string after round-trip for native-JSON values. Edge `from`/`to` carry the JSON-encoded value itself, which can therefore be of any JSON type.

Versioning policy: the schema is versioned via its URL path segment (`/v1/`). Non-breaking additions (new optional properties, new enum values) happen in-place on v1. Breaking changes (removed/renamed fields, tightened constraints, changed value shapes) ship as `/v2/graph.schema.json` with a new `$id`; v1 remains frozen. Instances MAY self-identify via the optional `schema_version` field; absence means v1 by convention.

schema_version.description: Self-identification of the graph instance's schema major version. Must equal `"1"` for documents conforming to this schema; producers of future v2 documents will emit `"2"` and be validated by `/v2/graph.schema.json`.

roots.description: Seed values for the BFS, in input order. Duplicates are not filtered (iterate passes list(start) through). Items may be of any JSON type.

nodes.description: Map from a string key (the node's identity, derived from str(value) on the producer side) to node metadata.

edges.description: Real edges. Deduplicated on (from, to) — if two rules fire the same op from x to the same successor, only the first is recorded.

pseudo_edges.description: Edges that would have fired but were suppressed by Rule.bound returning False or by max_depth halting expansion. No `to` field — the successor is not materialized. Deduplicated on (from, op).

op_order.description: Distinct op ids in declaration order: rules first (first-seen wins), then `default.id` if not already present. Drives palette assignment in to_dot.

op_labels.description: Map from op id to the display label rendered on its edges. The id is the stable key (used in `op_order`, `edge.op`, and op_colors pins); label is cosmetic. Optional: when omitted, consumers SHOULD fall back to displaying the id itself.

depth.description: Minimum BFS hop count from the nearest root. Roots have depth 0.

key_type.description: JSON-native type of the original node value before JSON stringification. One of the seven JSON Schema primitives (`null`, `boolean`, `integer`, `number`, `string`, `array`, `object`) so consumers in any language can interpret it without Python-specific knowledge. Drives type-sensitive rendering (e.g. skipping `show_factors` for non-integer values) instead of inferring type from the string form of the key. Python-side mapping: `bool` → `boolean`, `int` → `integer`, `float` → `number`, `str` → `string`, `list`/`tuple`/`set`/`frozenset` → `array`, `dict` → `object`, `None` → `null`; anything else is coerced to `string` via the default `str()` JSON encoder.

tags.description: Names of tag predicates that matched this value. Only present when non-empty. The conventional name `highlight` triggers the renderer's fill-darkening.

op.description: Op id — must appear in the graph's op_order. The id is the stable color/ordering key; display label for this edge is looked up in `op_labels` (falling back to the id itself).
Op id — must appear in the graph's op_order.
